zh.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam是Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm和von Achalm的兒子，於大約1005年出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀。他和Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於大約1050年享年47歲，埋葬在法國阿爾薩斯巴-爾斯特拉斯布-維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam是Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm和von Achalm的儿子，于大约1005年出生在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀。他和Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于大约1050年享年47岁，埋葬在法国阿爾薩斯巴-爾斯特拉斯布-維爾。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Dettingen,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name for someone from any of numerous places called Dettingen.

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Urach,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Cheskel, Moshe, Chaim, Mendel, Pinchas, Bernat, Hershel, Meyer, Moche, Mort, Rivka, Rivky.

German: topographic name from Middle High German brache ‘fallow land, pastureland’, originally ‘newly plowed land’. Alternatively, a habitational name from any of the places called Braach (Hesse) or Brach (Baden-Württemberg, Franconia).
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Barach  .

variants.name: Breach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal.
English (Sussex): variant of   Brach  .

variants.name: Brecher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Chaim, Yosef, Baila, Chana, Ephraim, Herschel, Hershel, Meir, Meyer, Pnina, Shimon.  German Armin, Oskar, Bernd, Erwin, Gerhard.
English: variant of Brach 2, from Middle English breche, Old English brǣc, brēc ‘newly cultivated land’, the -er suffix denoting an inhabitant.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from an agent derivative of German brechen ‘to break’, an occupational name for someone who crushed hemp or flax, or a nickname for a law breaker or evildoer.

variants.name: Bracher
meanings: English: from an agent derivative of Middle English brech ‘land broken up for cultivation’ (Old English brǣc, brēc), denoting a dweller by newly cultivated land. Compare Brach 2 and Breach .
Swiss German: variant of German Brachmann (see Brachman ).
German: variant of   Brach   1.

variants.name: Brachman
meanings: Americanized form of German Brachmann: variant of Brach , with the addition of Middle High German man ‘man’.

variants.name: Brech
meanings: German: variant of   Brach   1 or   Brecht  .
English: variant of   Breach   (see   Brach   2).

variants.name: Barach
meanings: Americanized form of Croatian and Serbian Barać and Barač (see Barac ).
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): acronymic surname from the first letters of the Hebrew patronymic phrase Ben Rabi Hayyim ‘son of rabbi Hayyim’.

nameMeanings.name: Egino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Junichi, Yoshihiro, Haruo, Hideo, Hiroaki, Kaori, Katsumi, Kazuhide, Kazuhiko, Koichi, Maki, Masae.
Japanese: written 荻野 ‘reed plain’, of which Japan has many. It is found mostly in eastern Japan.

nameMeanings.name: II.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Achlam
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from either Acklam (ER Yorks) or West Acklam (NR Yorks), both of which are recorded as Aclun in 1086, Acclum in the 12th century. The etymology of the place-names is uncertain. They possibly contain Old Scandinavian * ak(k)ul- ‘ankle’ (used in Norwegian place-names with the sense ‘slope’) in the dative plural form * ak(k)lum, i.e. ‘(place) at the slopes’. Alternatively, they may derive from an Old English * āc-lēah ‘oak wood, oak-tree clearing’ in the dative plural form * āc-lēum, i.e. ‘(place) at the oak-tree clearings’.

variants.name: Ackland
meanings: English:
habitational name from Acland Barton in Landkey (Devon), named with the Old English personal name Acca + Old English lanu ‘lane’, meaning ‘Acca's lane’.
(North Midlands): variant of Acklam, a habitational name from either Acklam or West Acklam (Yorkshire).

nameMeanings.name: Egino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the many given names ending in -gino, e.g. Giorgino, pet form of Giorgio , and Luigino, pet form of Luigi .

en.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam was born about 1005, in Urach, Urach, Württemberg, Germany as the son of Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm and von Achalm. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden. He died about 1050, at the age of 47, and was buried in Strasbourg-Ville, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam est né environ 1005, à Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Allemagne, fils de Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm et von Achalm. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden. Il est décédé environ 1050, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Strasbourg-Ville, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, France.

de.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam wurde um 1005 in Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deutschland, als Sohn von Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm und von Achalm geboren. Er hatte mit Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1050 im Alter von 47 Jahren und wurde in Straßburg, Niederrhein, Elsaß, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam è nato il circa 1005 nel Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Germania come figlio di Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm e von Achalm. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden. Morì circa 1050, all'età di 47 anni, e fu sepolto a Strasbourg-Ville, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlamは約1005年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチでLiutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalmとvon Achalmの息子として生まれた。彼はKunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfeldenとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は約1050年に47歳で死去し，フランスアルサスバス-ルヒンストラスボウルグ-ヴィルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam(은)는 대략 1005년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 라치 라치에서 Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm(와)과 von Achalm의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1050년 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 알사스 바스-히 스트라스보우르그-빌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam nasceu aproximadamente 1005, em Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemanha filho de Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm e von Achalm. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1050, com 47 anos, e foi sepultado em Strasbourg-Ville, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam родился около 1005 г. в местности урэич, обирамт урэич, вuрттембирг, Германия. Его родителей звали Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm и von Achalm. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden. Он умер около 1050 г. в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности страсбург-вилл, бас-рин, алсеис, Франция.

sm.um 1005–um 1050: e tusa o le 1005–e tusa o le 1050

es.lifeSummary: Egino I. von Dettingen, Graf von Urach,  Egino II. von Achlam nació aproximadamente 1005, en Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemania como hijo de Liutold von Dettingen, Graf von Achalm y von Achalm. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Kunigunde Hadwich von Rheinfelden. Murió aproximadamente 1050, a la edad de 47 años, y fue sepultado en Strasbourg-Ville, Bas-Rhin, Alsace, Francia.
